profile:
Open Numerics builds large-scale quantitative computing tools for R&D and engineering teams working on scientific and industrial modeling problems. Its core services are: scalable quantitative modeling and simulation; machine learning/AI for scientific data; custom algorithms and numerical solvers; uncertainty quantification and probabilistic inference; GPU/CUDA and HPC integration; and onsite, private-cloud, hosted or containerized deployments. ([opennumerics.com](https://opennumerics.com/))

Technically, ON works with PDE-based modeling and simulation, finite elements via FEniCS, finite volumes via OpenFOAM, custom solvers, deterministic and stochastic systems, multiscale formulations, hybrid physics–ML models, scientific machine learning, surrogate models, customized ML optimizers, Bayesian inference, stochastic sampling, adaptive time-stepping, particle-based methods, CUDA kernels, parallel solvers, Slurm-based ML workflows and large-scale simulation pipelines. ([opennumerics.com](https://opennumerics.com/))

The site positions ON for problems where first-principles simulation, machine learning and uncertainty estimates must be integrated: accelerating PDE solvers with ML surrogates or foundation models; estimating physical parameters from experimental data; design optimization; digital twins; rare-event and out-of-sample risk; and deploying simulation or ML services into operational workflows. It names biotech, pharma, energy and aerospace as target industries. ([opennumerics.com](https://opennumerics.com/))

Examples on the site include generative AI for battery porous-media transport, PDE-constrained optimization for chemical-reactor control, and Bayesian optimization/UQ for estimating elastic material parameters from measurement data. ([opennumerics.com](https://opennumerics.com/solutions/sgm.html))
